name: pgvectorscale
tagline: A DiskANN-based index that scales pgvector search on PostgreSQL
seoTitle: pgvectorscale: Faster pgvector Search for Postgres
metaDescription: pgvectorscale adds a StreamingDiskANN index and binary quantization to pgvector, giving PostgreSQL faster, cheaper vector search for AI apps.
overview: pgvectorscale is a PostgreSQL extension that complements pgvector, the open-source vector data extension for Postgres. It adds a StreamingDiskANN index type, inspired by Microsoft's DiskANN algorithm, along with Statistical Binary Quantization for compact storage and label-based filtered vector search.

It is meant for application developers and DBAs who already store embeddings in PostgreSQL with pgvector and want approximate nearest neighbor search to stay fast and affordable as data grows into the tens of millions of vectors. You keep your data in Postgres instead of moving it to a separate vector database.

Unlike pgvector, which is written in C, pgvectorscale is built in Rust using the PGRX framework. It installs as the `vectorscale` extension and works alongside pgvector, so you create indexes and run queries with familiar SQL and pgvector distance operators.
features: StreamingDiskANN index, a disk-friendly graph index inspired by Microsoft's DiskANN research
Statistical Binary Quantization for compact storage and faster search than standard binary quantization
Label-based filtered vector search that combines similarity search with label filtering
Works as a complement to pgvector, reusing its VECTOR type and distance operators (<=>, <->, <#>)
Self-hosted on your own PostgreSQL, available via Docker, source build, or Timescale Cloud
Written in Rust with PGRX, giving the Postgres community another way to contribute to vector support

gettingStarted.intro: Install the extension, enable it in your database, then create a StreamingDiskANN index on an embedding column and query it with pgvector operators.

steps.heading: Run PostgreSQL with the extension
steps.body: The quickest path is the TimescaleDB Docker image, which bundles pgvectorscale. Then connect with psql.

steps.heading: Create the extension
steps.body: Enable vectorscale. CASCADE automatically installs pgvector as well.
steps.code: CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vectorscale CASCADE;

steps.heading: Create a table with an embedding column
steps.body: Store your embeddings in a VECTOR column using the pgvector type.

steps.heading: Add a StreamingDiskANN index and query it
steps.body: Create a diskann index, then run a nearest neighbor search with the cosine distance operator.

useCases: Scaling an existing pgvector setup to tens of millions of embeddings while keeping low query latency
Keeping vector search inside PostgreSQL instead of running a separate vector database
Combining vector similarity with label filtering for more precise retrieval in a RAG pipeline
Reducing storage cost for large embedding tables using binary quantization
